LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA/KOCH/TIDEWATER JOINT VENTURE'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE TRIAL C.C.P. 597 AND 598 IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BE RENOTICED BEFORE THE TRIAL JUDGE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA/KOCH/TIDEWATER'S (A JOINT VENUTRE) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE, BUT IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO HIS STATUS AS A SEAMAN GIVEN HIS DUTIES AND ACTIVITIES IN THE MONTHS SURROUNDING AND AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT AND ALL THE OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE. THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER THE VESSEL WAS UNSEAWORTHY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF RICHARD WARNECKE'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7 AND PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1008(D) IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA/KOCH/TIDEWATER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS AT LEAST A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE ACTIVITY THAT INJURED PLAINTIFF WAS IN DEFENDANT'S CAPACITY AS A VESSEL OWNER AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1350 FOR OBTAINING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF INDIVIDUAL CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189 (301/PJB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF RICHARD WARNECKE'S Motion For Sanctions UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7 AND PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1008(D) IS DENIED. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/BD

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA/KOCH/TIDEWATER, A JOINT VENUTRE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE, BUT IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO HIS STATUS AS A SEAMAN GIVEN HIS DUTIES AND ACTIVITIES IN THE MONTHS SURROUNDING AND AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT AND ALL THE OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE CASE. THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER THE VESSEL WAS UNSEAWORTHY. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/BD

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
MAX LEE KELLEY, ESQ.(COX, WOOTTON, GRIFFIN, HANSEN & POULOS, LLP), (415)438-4600, FOR DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA/KOCH/TIDEWATER
CHRISTOPHER A. VIADRO, ESQ.(THE VEEN FIRM, PC), (415)673-4800, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, RICHARD WARNECKE

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT TUTOR-SALIBA/KOCH/TIDEWATER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS AT LEAST A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE ACTIVITY THAT INJURED PLAINTIFF WAS IN DEFENDANT'S CAPACITY AS A VESSEL OWNER AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1350 FOR OBTAINING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF INDIVIDUAL CAUSES OF ACTION.
